label: Overall
title: Grade A · 35/40
subtitle: Our grading
summary: Overall score from the eight dimensions, plus the letter bucket it lands in.

facts.label: Score
facts.value: 35/40 · Grade A

facts.label: Dimensions
facts.value: Perk Optimality · Curio Efficiency · Talent Coherence · Blessing Synergy · Role Coverage · Breakpoint Relevance · Difficulty Scaling · Survivability

facts.label: Scaling rule
facts.value: If a dimension was unscorable, the composite scales up from the dimensions we could score so missing data does not force a lower letter.

facts.label: Implies
facts.value: Two builds with the same composite can look very different — same sum, different shape. The grade is a starting point; the dimensions are the story.

facts.label: Caveat
facts.value: Our grading, not community consensus. The cutoffs are calibrated against the 24 fixture meta builds.

label: Perks
title: Perk Optimality
subtitle: 5/5 Exemplary
summary: How close your weapon perks are to max rolls, averaged across weapons.

facts.label: Score
facts.value: 5/5 — Exemplary

facts.label: Range
facts.value: 1 (low-tier rolls) to 5 (all T4 or near)

facts.label: Derivation
facts.value: Each perk snaps to its nearest tier 1–4, averaged per weapon, then across weapons.

facts.label: This build
facts.value: 2 weapons · avg perk tier 4/4

facts.label: Implies
facts.value: Near-max rolls across the board — no breakpoint left on the table from sub-tier perks.

facts.label: Caveat
facts.value: Judges roll quality, not perk choice. A T4 perk on the wrong stat still scores well here; Role Coverage and Breakpoint Relevance catch that.

label: Curios
title: Curio Efficiency
subtitle: 4/5 Strong
summary: Whether your curio perks fit this class's usual toolkit, and whether they're rolled high.

facts.label: Score
facts.value: 4/5 — Strong

facts.label: Range
facts.value: 1 (an 'avoid' perk present) to 5 (all class-optimal, all near-max)

facts.label: Derivation
facts.value: Each curio perk gets a class-specific rating (optimal / solid / neutral / avoid) plus a tier 1–4 from its roll value.

facts.label: This build
facts.value: 4 optimal · 6 solid · 2 neutral · 0 avoid · avg tier 4/4

facts.label: Implies
facts.value: Most are class-appropriate; some are sub-tier or off-profile.

facts.label: Caveat
facts.value: Our per-class ratings are a starting point. A niche curio line can still make sense for a specific play pattern.

label: Talents
title: Talent Coherence
subtitle: 4/5 Strong
summary: How tightly your talents feed each other — do they stack the same stats or chain into each other, or do they work in isolation?

facts.label: Score
facts.value: 4/5 — Strong

facts.label: Range
facts.value: 1 (scattered picks) to 5 (tightly amplifying core)

facts.label: Derivation
facts.value: Count talent-to-talent synergies, divide by talent count, then adjust for focus and talents that do not interact with anything.

facts.label: This build
facts.value: 26 talents · 12 synergy pairs · 0 standalone picks

facts.label: Implies
facts.value: Nearly every talent either stacks the same category or chains into another pick — the build has a clear thesis.

facts.label: Important caveat
facts.value: Only scores synergies the simulator can see. Teammate-coherency buffs, positional play, and narrative talents can work even when they do not produce visible synergy pairs.

label: Blessings
title: Blessing Synergy
subtitle: 5/5 Exemplary
summary: Whether your blessings feed your talents and each other, or sit alone on their weapons.

facts.label: Score
facts.value: 5/5 — Exemplary

facts.label: Range
facts.value: 1 (blessings work in isolation) to 5 (deeply woven in)

facts.label: Derivation
facts.value: Count synergies that involve at least one blessing, per blessing. Bonus if two blessings cooperate; penalty per blessing that does not interact with anything.

facts.label: This build
facts.value: 4 blessings · 36 synergies · 0 with no interaction

facts.label: Implies
facts.value: Blessings amplify your talents or each other.

facts.label: Caveat
facts.value: A blessing that is perfect for its weapon but has no visible synergy with your talents gets no credit here. That is a modeling limit, not necessarily a build flaw.

label: Role
title: Role Coverage
subtitle: 5/5 Exemplary
summary: How broad your build is across stat categories, and whether it has any obvious gaps.

facts.label: Score
facts.value: 5/5 — Exemplary

facts.label: Range
facts.value: 1 (very narrow plus a clear gap) to 5 (broad coverage, no gaps)

facts.label: Derivation
facts.value: Count the active stat categories out of 11, then subtract for any named gap and for severe melee/ranged imbalance.

facts.label: This build
facts.value: 9/11 categories active · balanced

facts.label: Implies
facts.value: Broad coverage, no flagged gaps.

label: Breakpoints
title: Breakpoint Relevance
subtitle: 5/5 Exemplary
summary: How many of our Damnation breakpoint checklist entries your build hits, weighted by importance.

facts.label: Score
facts.value: 5/5 — Exemplary

facts.label: Range
facts.value: 1 (most checklist breakpoints missed) to 5 (nearly all met)

facts.label: Derivation
facts.value: For each checklist entry, we find your best hits-to-kill across all weapons at that enemy, hit location, and scenario.

facts.label: This build
facts.value: 10/10 breakpoints met

facts.label: Implies
facts.value: You hit the muscle-memory breakpoints — one-shot Trapper/Hound head, two-hit Crusher, two-hit Rager body, and similar anchors.

facts.label: Caveat
facts.value: Our checklist, not the game's. The list is curated for decisive breakpoints, not exhaustive coverage of every matchup.

facts.label: Coverage caveat
facts.value: If a weapon family is not fully modelled yet, the score can under-credit it. The per-weapon breakpoint panels may show Unsupported.

label: Scaling
title: Difficulty Scaling
subtitle: 4/5 Strong
summary: Do your key breakpoints still hold at Auric, or do they break down on the harder difficulty?

facts.label: Score
facts.value: 4/5 — Strong

facts.label: Range
facts.value: 1 (breaks at Damnation already) to 5 (holds through Auric)

facts.label: Derivation
facts.value: Tracks only the high-priority breakpoints and compares whether they still land at Damnation versus Auric.

facts.label: This build
facts.value: High-priority breakpoints met: 3/3 at Damnation · 2/3 at Auric · breaks at Auric: Two-hit Crusher

facts.label: Implies
facts.value: Holds at Damnation; some key breakpoints break at Auric.

facts.label: Important caveat
facts.value: Auric is our proxy for high-intensity play. Havoc layers extra resistance modifiers we do not model — holding at Auric is a good sign, not a guarantee.

label: Survivability
title: Survivability
subtitle: 3/5 Solid
summary: How much tougher this build is than the same class stripped back to baseline stats and default sustain.

facts.label: Score
facts.value: 3/5 — Solid

facts.label: Range
facts.value: 1 (near baseline) to 5 (stacked durability and recovery)

facts.label: Derivation
facts.value: Compares your effective HP, dodge/slide toughness, and sustain loops against a class-baseline build at Damnation.

facts.label: This build
facts.value: EHP x1.30 · movement toughness x2.48 · recovery x1.61

facts.label: Implies
facts.value: Defensive investment is real, but not carrying the whole build.

facts.label: Caveat
facts.value: This is class-relative, not raw HP worship. Ogryn bulk does not auto-win, and niche enemy-specific DR lines are still under-modeled here.
